##NAMES=Rosemaryite
##RRUFFID=R140519
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=[box]NaMn^2+^(Fe^3+^Al)(PO_4_)_3_
##LOCALITY=Buranga pegmatite, Gatumba District, Western Province, Rwanda
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=Rock Currier
##DESCRIPTION=Vitreous-opaque dark brown irregularly shaped masses intergrown with vitreous light brown childrenite and blocky dark blue scorzalite in a grayish siliceous matrix.
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R140519
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=(Na_0.49_Ca_0.05_Mn^2+^_0.29_[box]_0.17_)Mn^2+^_1.00_(Fe^3+^_0.82_Mg_0.10_Mn^2+^_0.08_)(Al^3+^_0.76_Fe^3+^_0.24_)(PO_4_)_3_
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 11.9673 b: 12.391 c: 6.3218 alpha: 90 beta: 114.4145 gamma: 90 volume: 853.61 crystal system: monoclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman Processed
